title: PAF Strikes 41 Afghan Locations Including Brigade HQs in Nangarhar and Kandahar
detail: Pakistani Air Force jets struck 41 locations across Afghanistan on March 30, targeting brigade and battalion headquarters in Nangarhar province and an army headquarters in Kandahar province. Heavy artillery exchanges resumed along the Kunar-Bajaur border with both Afghan and Pakistani forces firing across the Durand Line. Afghanistan's deputy spokesperson Hamdullah Fitrat confirmed Pakistan's shelling killed at least 1 person and wounded 16 others, predominantly women and children, in Kunar province. Pakistan denied deliberately targeting civilians, asserting its strikes were directed at Taliban military infrastructure and in response to cross-border Afghan fire. Modern Diplomacy noted the escalating strikes were complicating Pakistan's attempts to play a mediating role in US-Iran diplomatic overtures.

title: UN Human Rights Chief Türk: Af-Pak Conflict 'Piles Misery on Misery', Pleads for Dialogue
detail: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk issued a formal statement condemning the Afghanistan-Pakistan conflict, calling it a situation that 'piles misery on misery' for civilian populations already suffering from food insecurity, displacement, and decades of war. Türk called for immediate dialogue and de-escalation, noting that OHCHR had documented hundreds of civilian casualties since fighting intensified on February 26. The statement cited ongoing cross-border shelling in Kunar, Nangarhar, Khost, and Paktika as causing disproportionate civilian harm. Türk urged both Pakistan and the Taliban government to respect international humanitarian law and called on regional powers including China, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Qatar to intensify mediation efforts. The statement represented the UN's strongest formal condemnation of the conflict to date.
